danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental traps visible in stage design: spiked walls, landmines, burning ropes, slick floors
danger_signals.meaning: Active danger present in arena; character vulnerability to environmental damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Character punch/kick startup frames and impact effects
danger_signals.meaning: Incoming threat; need to block or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Character pose change and visual effect burst when ABC buttons pressed simultaneously
danger_signals.meaning: High-damage attack incoming; immediate danger escalation

safety_signals.visual_form: Character stun animation or knockback duration
safety_signals.meaning: Brief window of safety to attack or reposition

safety_signals.visual_form: Screen space separation; character position relative to opponent
safety_signals.meaning: Safety proportional to distance; projectile-only threats at range

safety_signals.visual_form: Character defensive stance with blocking visual
safety_signals.meaning: Damage reduction active; reduced immediate threat

faction_indicators.faction: Historical World Heroes (Dr. Brown's Tournament Participants)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All tournament participants are visually distinct historical/fictional characters (no faction color coding detected)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament arena stages; neutral battlegrounds representing various historical locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clean arena environments; tournament branding; formal combat presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard fighting rules; 1v1 combat structure; no environmental interference

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Traps integrated into stage terrain: visible spiked walls, burning elements, ice floors, landmine markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental hazards damage both fighters; trap placement affects movement and positioning strategy; increased tactical complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ABC button combination triggers character-specific visual effect burst and pose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Special moves activate with ABC simultaneous press; vary by character (fake moves, projectile catch, counter abilities); require tactical timing

environmental_storytelling.example: Fighters are based on actual historical figures and fictional World Heroes; visual appearance (clothing, weapon style, movement) communicates time period and cultural origin

Different arena hazards (spiked walls, burning ropes, landmines, slick floors) communicate environmental storytelling through obstacle design; suggests diverse historical/thematic locations for the time-machine tournament
Dr. Brown's time-machine premise visually established through stage design and character selection screen; tournament framing creates meta-narrative context for why diverse historical figures compete

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can perform: punch (hold for strength), kick (hold for strength), throw; 3-button input scheme (later games allow 4-button reassignment)
ABC simultaneous button press triggers character-unique special move; varies dramatically (Fuuma: fake special, J. Max: projectile catch/throw, Kim Dragon: counter while blocking)
Must navigate and avoid environmental hazards; traps cause damage; positioning becomes critical vs. static tournament mode
Spiked walls damage on contact; landmines trigger on proximity/stepping; burning ropes inflict fire damage; slick floors reduce traction/control

notes: World Heroes uses minimalist visual communication focused on character distinction and arena hazards rather than faction/political systems. The time-machine tournament premise is the primary world-state framework. Danger signals are primarily mechanical (animations, hazards) rather than color-coded or symbolic. No faction allegiances or political alignments detected in source material. Character visual design carries historical/cultural identity but not faction identity. Death Match mode creates state-dependent visual manifestation through trap placement.
